terms.definition: A sealed perspex box used to deliver volatile anaesthetic gas for induction in fractious or small patients.
terms.exampleSentence: The feral cat was induced in the induction chamber to allow safe handling.
terms.contextNotes: Last-resort technique; ensure adequate scavenging in the room.

terms.definition: A non-invasive device measuring peripheral haemoglobin oxygen saturation and pulse rate via a probe on the tongue or pinna.
terms.exampleSentence: The pulse oximeter showed an SpO2 of 96 per cent throughout anaesthesia.
terms.contextNotes: Pigmented tongues and vasoconstriction reduce reliability.

terms.definition: The breathing system delivering oxygen and volatile agent to the patient and removing exhaled carbon dioxide.
terms.exampleSentence: A T-piece anaesthetic circuit was selected for the kitten under three kilograms.
terms.contextNotes: Choice depends on patient body weight and fresh-gas flow.

terms.definition: A protective conical collar worn by an animal to prevent it from licking or chewing wounds and surgical sites.
terms.exampleSentence: Keep the Elizabethan collar on at all times until the sutures are removed.
terms.contextNotes: Soft alternatives exist but may be less effective for posterior wounds.

terms.definition: A device fitted over an animal's snout to prevent biting during examination, transport, or handling.
terms.exampleSentence: A basket muzzle was placed before approaching the dog for venepuncture.
terms.contextNotes: Basket type allows panting and is preferred for any restraint over a few minutes.

terms.definition: A handheld radio-frequency reader used to detect and display the unique identification number of an implanted microchip.
terms.exampleSentence: Pass the microchip scanner over the shoulders, sides, and base of the neck systematically.
terms.contextNotes: Use a universal-frequency scanner to detect older non-ISO chips.

terms.definition: Magnifying optical lenses worn during dental and surgical procedures to improve visualisation of fine structures.
terms.exampleSentence: Dental loupes are essential when locating fine root remnants during extractions.
terms.contextNotes: Match working distance to clinician posture to avoid neck strain.

terms.definition: A high-pressure steam sterilising chamber used to render surgical instruments and consumables sterile.
terms.exampleSentence: Run the autoclave on the standard wrapped instrument cycle and verify the indicator strip.
terms.contextNotes: Daily and weekly validation logs are required for clinical governance.

terms.definition: A dedicated, controlled-access area within the practice comprising preparation, theatre, and recovery zones.
terms.exampleSentence: Move the patient through to the surgical suite once it is intubated.
terms.contextNotes: Maintain positive pressure and restrict throughput to reduce contamination.

terms.definition: A documented agreement in which the owner is informed of risks, benefits, costs, and alternatives before the procedure.
terms.exampleSentence: Please ensure informed consent for surgery is signed and the cost estimate is on file before pre-medication.
terms.contextNotes: Verbal consent is insufficient for elective procedures.

terms.definition: A structured, empathetic conversation with the owner about prognosis, quality of life, and euthanasia options.
terms.exampleSentence: I would like to schedule an end-of-life discussion this week to plan ahead for Bella.
terms.contextNotes: Allow extended consultation time and a private room.

terms.definition: A written breakdown of expected fees provided to the owner before non-emergency treatment or surgery.
terms.exampleSentence: Please review and sign the cost estimate; the figure is exclusive of VAT.
terms.contextNotes: Indicate that the figure is an estimate and explain why it may change.

terms.definition: Onward transfer of a case to a clinician with advanced certification when it exceeds the primary practice's scope.
terms.exampleSentence: I am arranging a referral to a specialist in veterinary cardiology for further imaging.
terms.contextNotes: Send full clinical history, imaging, and laboratory data with the referral.

terms.definition: A structured clinical record comprising Subjective findings, Objective data, Assessment, and Plan.
terms.exampleSentence: Please complete the SOAP notes before handover at the end of the shift.
terms.contextNotes: Plan section should include diagnostics, therapeutics, and client communication.

terms.definition: Examination of an animal carcass after death to determine cause of death or extent of disease.
terms.exampleSentence: With the owner's consent, a post-mortem was performed and tissues submitted for histopathology.
terms.contextNotes: Document owner consent in the clinical record.

terms.definition: An infectious disease that can be naturally transmitted from vertebrate animals to humans.
terms.exampleSentence: Leptospirosis is an important zoonosis, particularly in occupationally exposed owners.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel owners about hand hygiene and exposure-control measures.

terms.definition: Transmission of an infectious disease from humans to animals; also termed anthroponosis or zooanthroponosis.
terms.exampleSentence: Reverse zoonosis was suspected after the household member tested positive for SARS-CoV-2.
terms.contextNotes: Owners with respiratory illness should limit close contact with companion animals.

terms.definition: Coordinated practice ensuring the responsible selection, dose, and duration of antimicrobials to preserve efficacy.
terms.exampleSentence: Our antimicrobial stewardship policy mandates culture and sensitivity before dispensing fluoroquinolones.
terms.contextNotes: Aligned with PROTECT ME and BSAVA guidelines in the UK.

terms.definition: Procedural and physical measures designed to prevent the introduction and spread of infectious agents in a facility.
terms.exampleSentence: Strict biosecurity is required when admitting suspected parvovirus cases.
terms.contextNotes: Includes PPE, foot dips, and dedicated equipment for isolation cases.

terms.definition: Isolation nursing technique using dedicated PPE and equipment to prevent cross-infection between patients.
terms.exampleSentence: Barrier nursing was instituted for the suspected leptospirosis case in the isolation ward.
terms.contextNotes: Don and doff PPE in a defined order; document each entry.

terms.definition: Written and verbal guidance given to the owner at collection, covering medication, monitoring, and follow-up.
terms.exampleSentence: Go through the discharge instructions in detail and confirm that the owner understands the medication schedule.
terms.contextNotes: Provide both written and verbal copies; check teach-back understanding.

terms.definition: An integrated approach recognising the interdependence of human, animal, and environmental health.
terms.exampleSentence: Antimicrobial resistance is best tackled through a One Health framework involving veterinary and medical colleagues.
terms.contextNotes: Endorsed by WHO, OIE (WOAH), and FAO as the standard cross-sector model.

terms.definition: A disease that, by law, must be reported to the relevant government authority on suspicion or confirmation.
terms.exampleSentence: Suspected rabies is a notifiable disease and APHA must be informed immediately.
terms.contextNotes: Failure to report carries criminal penalties under the Animal Health Act.

terms.definition: The structured transfer of clinical responsibility and patient information from one shift or clinician to the next.
terms.exampleSentence: The clinical handover at eight o'clock should cover all hospitalised patients using SBAR.
terms.contextNotes: SBAR (Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation) is widely adopted.

terms.definition: Clothing and devices worn by staff to reduce exposure to infectious, chemical, or radiological hazards.
terms.exampleSentence: Don full personal protective equipment before entering the isolation kennel.
terms.contextNotes: Includes gloves, gown, mask, and eye protection for high-risk cases.
